title: Campylotropis hirtella
intro: Campylotropis hirtella, also known as hairy Campylotropis, is a type of legume plant in the Fabaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Campylotropis hirtella include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Campylotropis hirtella is often made into tea, decoctions, or infusions, while its extracts are also used in capsule or tablet form, making it a popular herbal preparation for treating various health conditions.
taxonomy: Campylotropis hirtella is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Campylotropis. The genus Campylotropis is part of the subfamily Faboideae, which contains legumes. Campylotropis hirtella is native to China and has been classified as a shrub or a small tree. It is a relatively rare species, and its classification is primarily based on its botanical characteristics.
